TextENGNew: ...That day, it rained from the afternoon onward and never stopped.
Tilting my umbrella slightly, I looked up at the sky.

Dull, murky rain clouds blanketed everything, and countless raindrops hammered the ground without end. Perhaps because the peak of summer was drawing near, the air clung to my skin — thick, heavy, and oppressive.
With every step through the puddles, water seeped into my school-regulation loafers, and my socks grew miserably sodden.
...I'd never liked rainy days.
It was just a natural phenomenon — water falling from the sky — and yet the moment it rained, my spirits sank all on their own.
Back in Okinomiya, I'd always been told that farmers needed the rain. But for someone like me who'd never done a day's farm work, rain was nothing but a nuisance that cancelled fun activities.
The crowds moved back and forth, everyone hiding their faces behind their umbrellas. Even when they bumped into someone or water splashed on them, they never so much as looked at the other person.
All anyone could see was the narrow sliver of space directly ahead of them.
That was why, on rainy days, everyone and everything around me became nothing more than obstacles. The same route felt so much longer than usual, and so unbearably dreary...
"...Haah."
Another sigh. ...Come to think of it, I'd been sighing constantly about everything lately. The intervals were getting shorter, and the breaths heavier.
It was like a load strapped to my back that grew a little heavier with each passing day. ...Sick of my own pessimism, I was disgusted with myself.
Since that day... the cold war between Grandma and Mom had been going on for over a week.
Mom refused to exchange a single word with Grandma, and Grandma was too stubborn to make any attempt at reconciliation.
Grandma had always been gentle and mild, but... she could be inflexible to the point of sheer obstinacy. This standoff would likely continue for a while yet.

And at times like these... Dad, far from mediating, was too spineless to even go near them. So instead, I was the one forced between them as a go-between — treated like nothing more than a human buffer.
"Why is it always me..."
I muttered bitterly under my breath.
I wasn't a message board or a tape recorder. Just relaying their words to each other as instructed was mentally exhausting... It wore me down.
I was frustrated too. I wanted to lash out somewhere, and I wished desperately that someone else could take my place.
And yet they treated me like a child every other time, only to use me as their errand girl the moment it was convenient — how was that anything but unfair...?
A heavy, turbid sediment continued to build in my chest, dragging my spirits down until I nearly broke into tears.
My beloved family... Yes — until that disaster, I'd been proud of this family.
My nagging but dependable Mom.
My meek but gentle Dad.
My old-fashioned but kind Grandma.
Everyone had their faults, myself included. But the virtues more than made up for them, so the little things never bothered me. I'd simply accepted it all as part of who they were.
But now those same faults had turned against us, tearing apart the family bonds we'd built...
"I hope things go back to normal soon..."

After voicing that wish, I let out the biggest sigh of the day — a deep breath that emptied my lungs completely. ...Then I lifted my head, and with a stride wider than my usual trudge, I pushed forward.
Today was Saturday. I'd missed last week because of final exams, but today was my shift at the care center.
All the residents there looked forward to seeing my cheerful face. I didn't want to worry them by dragging this dark mood along.
With no knowledge of caregiving and no experience, the only thing I could do was... simply work as hard as I could.

I took the train and arrived at the center.@k ...A small break in the rain clouds let through a patch of brighter sky, and it gave me a little relief.@k This truly was... the only place where I could find peace right now.
After getting changed, I entered the center's break room and furrowed my brow — no one was there.
The center was supposed to have a caregiver on standby at all times, ready to handle any situation.

That applied even after the residents went to bed, and breaks were taken in shifts. So the only times the break room emptied out were during the three daily meals and snack service, when all hands were needed.
"But that's odd.@k@vS04/36/310100607. It's not snack time yet..."
Saying that, I reached for the doorknob to go check the floor — and that was when it happened.
"——Stop this at once, Hatakeyama-san!!"
An angry shout erupted from beyond the door, making me flinch and jump back. And then—
"That voice just now... Yamase-san?"
More than the volume, I was stunned by who it came from. In all the time I'd been working here, I had never once seen the mild-mannered Yamase-san raise her voice in anger.
And... did she just say 'Hatakeyama-san'?
I rushed out of the break room and hurried toward the source of the voice.
...What greeted me was even more shocking: a crowd of people had gathered at the far end of the hallway, and all the residents were peering toward the back room from a cautious distance.
And that room was, just as I'd feared——!
"E-excuse me!@k@vS04/36/310100611. Please, let me through!"
Bowing my head, I carefully parted the crowd of elderly residents, using as little force as possible. When I finally reached the doorway — I gasped, frozen in place.
"Wh... what is this?!"
It was a bizarre sight.
The first thing that caught my eye when I stepped inside... were life-sized, human-shaped cutouts of white paper, hung from the windows and curtain rails.
They fluttered like Tanabata decorations... roughly six of them swaying together in the breeze from the air conditioning.
Looking more closely, I could see that one bore old man Hatakeyama's given name, while the others had different names written on them in what looked like ink.
But even more disturbing... were the crimson talismans plastered across every surface of the room's walls.
On the floor, too.
On the ceiling.
On every piece of furniture — not a single surface had been spared, all covered densely with the red paper.
There were so many it was frightening to imagine how they'd all been gathered. The room, which should have been white, was now drenched in crimson.
One of the talismans peeled loose and fluttered down to my feet. I picked it up gingerly. The red ink characters were partially smeared in an uneven brushstroke, spelling out a strange passage.
"With reverent awe we beseech the great god Oyashiro... grant purification... make clean and bright... hear our plea... in trembling reverence we declare..."
What was this...? Some kind of ritual prayer, or an incantation?
It was like a spell. And the name I could clearly make out within it was 'Oyashiro-sama.'
'Oyashiro-sama,' 'Oyashiro-sama'...
No — more than that... looking closely, this red was a strange, dark reddish-black, dry and powdery... and it carried a raw, metallic, nauseating stench——?!
"——Nooo!!"
I screamed in revulsion and flung the talisman away. The lukewarm, slick feel of the still-damp portion — it was blood!
I desperately wiped my hand on my apron, but a bone-deep chill raced from my spine through my entire body, and I couldn't stop the trembling — the vile sensation lingering in my fingertips.
And then, mingled with the murmur of voices from the hallway, those same strange words reached me again — seizing my heart like a claw. ...The very same bizarre incantation written on the talismans.

"...Upon this sacred altar... the brewed sake of offering... we present in reverence... with trembling awe we pray... grant peace and tranquility... hear us... great god Oyashiro... sublime and boundless..."
The one reciting it in a hoarse, flowing monotone was old man Hatakeyama...!
"With reverent awe we beseech the great god Oyashiro... grant purification... make clean and bright... hear our plea... in trembling reverence we declare..."
"Please stop!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1022. Can you hear me, Hatakeyama-san?!"

Another shrill cry brought me back to myself. In the center of the room, the old man knelt before a bed fashioned into something resembling an altar, offering his prayers — while Yamase-san stood beside him, voice raised in exasperation.

"Listen to me!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1024. Even if you're the only one occupying this room right now, you cannot just deface it like this without permission!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1025. Put everything back the way it was, immediately!!"

"...Upon this sacred altar... the brewed sake of offering... we present in reverence... with trembling awe we pray... grant peace and tranquility... hear us... great god Oyashiro... sublime and boundless..."
But no matter how many times Yamase-san's angry voice rang in his ears, old man Hatakeyama continued his prayers with single-minded fervor, as if possessed.
Her patience finally spent, Yamase-san seized his arm roughly and tried to haul him to his feet.
That was dangerous...! Lifting him that way could dislocate his shoulder!
"?! Ya-Yamase-san!@k@vS04/36/310100617. Please, wait!"
Sensing something was deeply wrong, I rushed over and stopped her.

For an instant, she turned on me with eyes blazing with fury, and I instinctively shrank back — but the moment she recognized me, sanity seemed to return. Her expression softened, and she slowly released her grip.
"I-I'm sorry... I..."
Yamase-san steadied her breathing while smoothing her disheveled bangs.
Seeing that, the other caregivers who'd been standing frozen in the doorway finally relaxed and began dispersing the crowd of anxious, curious residents.
"Wh-what on earth happened?@k@vS04/36/310100619. And all of this..."
"...I don't know.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1028. I had the late shift today, so I only just arrived myself.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1029. ...But the others told me it's been like this since this morning..."
Still visibly shaken, Yamase-san surveyed the room in disbelief. ...Even without a second look, the interior was beyond abnormal — it was genuinely eerie.

"...Upon this sacred altar... the brewed sake of offering... we present in reverence... with trembling awe we pray... grant peace and tranquility... hear us... great god Oyashiro... sublime and boundless..."
And through it all, old man Hatakeyama kept murmuring that strange liturgical chant, kneeling with his head bowed, wholly consumed by prayer.
Yamase-san and I could only look at each other in helpless bewilderment.
Sir... Hatakeyama... sir...
What on earth was going on...? How had things come to this?
The last time I saw him, he wasn't like this at all. He'd smiled at me, happily sharing stories of the old days in Hinamizawa.
So... why had he changed so suddenly, so completely? I didn't understand — I just didn't understand...!
Even here... Hinamizawa was creeping in, invading further and further...! Stealing away my safe places, one by one......!!
"...Apparently it's been happening a lot."

"There are rumors that people from Hinamizawa have been disturbing others with erratic behavior and delusions...@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1032. I'd thought it couldn't possibly be true, but now that I've seen this with my own eyes..."
"That's not — that's not true——"
Just then.
"——Um, excuse me.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_kaigoFa1005. Yamase-san, there's a visitor..."
"Not now.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1034. I'm in the middle of something."
"But, it's——"
"Forgive the intrusion.@k@vS04/28/310500114. I won't take much of your time — I just need a moment of your cooperation."
From behind the caregiver stepped a woman in a red suit.
"Wh-who are you...?"
"I don't believe we've met.@k@vS04/28/310500116. ——I'm Detective Minai, from the Kakiuchi Police Station."
"Minai——@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1037. a detective...?!"

Tension flashed across Yamase-san's face. And I was sure I looked the same. Being asked for your 'cooperation' by the police — even with a clear conscience, it was impossible not to imagine the worst.
The detective seemed accustomed to that reaction. Without being either ingratiating or overbearing, she naturally produced her badge.
Her tone was calm... but her eyes were sharp, already studying old man Hatakeyama behind us.
"Actually, I'm here because I'd like to speak with Kazuma Hatakeyama-san, who resides in this facility.@k@vS04/28/310500118. Would you permit me to see him?"
"Wh-what is this regarding...?"

"It pertains to the Great Hinamizawa Disaster that occurred recently.@k@vS04/28/310500120. I've learned that Hatakeyama-san's family is originally from Hinamizawa village, and I was hoping to ask him a few questions."
I barely managed to stifle my gasp. Even so, I was seized by the sudden feeling of all my strength draining from my limbs.
Why... why was a detective asking about the Hinamizawa disaster...?!
I stole a cautious glance at Yamase-san beside me. ...Good — she didn't seem to have noticed my panic.
With a polite 'excuse me,' Detective Minai moved to step into the room. Yamase-san caught her and stepped firmly into her path, blocking her way.
"Th-this is a medical facility...@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1040. Even for the police, I must ask you to refrain from making unilateral demands without the consent of the individual and their family."

"Yes, of course.@k@vS04/28/310500122. ——Which is precisely why I obtained consent from Hatakeyama-san's family in advance.@k@vS04/28/310500123. Here is the signed authorization from his next of kin.@k@|@y@vS04/28/310500124. Please, feel free to verify it."
Detective Minai produced an envelope from her breast pocket and offered it smoothly. After reviewing its contents, Yamase-san gave a reluctant, defeated nod.
...This woman was terrifying...!
I couldn't suppress a shudder. Detective Minai had anticipated every possible objection and prepared countermeasures in advance...?!
"......Please make it brief."
"Thank you for your cooperation.@k@|@y@vS04/28/310500126. ——Fujita-kun, if you would."
"Yes, ma'am."

The subordinate detective standing beside Minai nodded, then gestured the caregiver aside with a glance before stationing himself at the entrance. Even I could tell that was meant to keep everyone else out.
"...Now then."
After a light cough, Detective Minai strode toward where old man Hatakeyama and I stood.
Then she lowered herself to one knee, matching the eye level of the old man as he crouched, face down, still muttering to himself.
"...You are Kazuma Hatakeyama-san, correct?"
As expected, the old man didn't respond. ...But Detective Minai showed no sign of concern, maintaining her gentle tone as she continued.

"I saw the Hinamizawa incident on the news.@k@vS04/28/310500130. I understand that over two thousand people lost their lives.@k@vS04/28/310500131. ...Such a sudden tragedy. I can only imagine the toll it must be taking on you."
"However, something about this disaster caught my attention.@k@vS04/28/310500133. ...There are rumors that the volcanic gas disaster was in fact the 'curse of Oyashiro-sama.' Is that correct?"
"The village's own guardian deity, Oyashiro-sama, supposedly annihilated the entire village in a single night.@k@vS04/28/310500135. ...What exactly does that mean?"
Old man Hatakeyama was still muttering as before... but his shoulder gave a distinct twitch.
Catching that, Detective Minai produced a photograph from her notebook and held it up where he could see.
I was too far away to make it out clearly... but it appeared to show a girl dressed in shrine maiden's robes.
"Do you recognize this photograph?@k@vS04/28/310500137. It was taken at a festival unique to Hinamizawa village — the 'Watanagashi' festival."
"On June 19th of this year, the date this was taken, you attended the festival and met this girl.@k@vS04/28/310500139. ——Isn't that right?"

"This girl — Rika Furude — was said to be the shrine maiden of Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS04/28/310500141. And on June 22nd, the day before the disaster, she was killed in a gruesome manner by an unknown assailant.@k@vS04/28/310500142. ...And you believe the curse struck because she was killed.@k@vS04/28/310500143. That it was triggered by the murder of the head of the Furude family."
"Incidentally, for five years running in Hinamizawa village, this so-called 'curse of Oyashiro-sama' has supposedly claimed one death and one disappearance each year."
"And this year as well, two victims appeared right on schedule.@k@vS04/28/310500146. Do you know anything about this?"
Then I noticed.
The old man who had refused to listen to anyone was now trembling, and the face that had been frozen in blankness moments ago was contorted with terror.
And his muttering — which had sounded like nothing but an incomprehensible incantation — was gradually being contaminated by something else.
"...Hatakeyama-san?"
"......don'...@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1041. ...don't know...!"
"You can't possibly not know.@k@vS04/28/310500149. After all, here you are, offering prayers to Oyashiro-sama."
"Following the ancient customs and rites of Hinamizawa village, you've constructed an altar to worship your guardian deity. ——Isn't that so?@k@vS04/28/310500151. Am I wrong?"
"I-I don't know... don't know!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1043. I don't know anything, anything!!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1044. ——?!"
The old man turned his face away as if to flee. But Detective Minai smoothly circled around to face him head-on.
——In that instant, from my angle in her blind spot, I finally got a clear look at the detective's face.
Her tone may have been gentle, but... her eyes were as sharp as a bird of prey's — literally interrogating her subject with a gaze that could pierce through stone...!
What was she after? Why? In the first place, curses were baseless superstition — what exactly was this detective trying to investigate?
And what did any of this have to do with old man Hatakeyama, who had been living far from Hinamizawa? If she started following that thread, then even I...
"...Whatever little you know is perfectly fine.@k@vS04/28/310500153. Any detail, no matter how small, would be enough."
"Could you tell me about Oyashiro-sama's curse?@k@vS04/28/310500155. And the real reason you and the other elders are so terrified that you seal your lips shut——"
"Stop!@k@vS04/36/310100624. Please, stop this!!"
Unable to bear watching Detective Minai bear down on the old man like a predator cornering its prey, I threw myself between them. I stood in front of Hatakeyama-san, shielding him.
"?@k@vS04/28/310500157. What... do you think you're doing?"
Detective Minai's brow furrowed as she tilted her head slightly. ...She was clearly displeased at being interrupted. But even so, I summoned every ounce of courage I had and spoke.

"This man——@k@vS04/36/310100626. Hatakeyama-san has been deeply hurt by this disaster!@k@vS04/36/310100627. If you're truly a detective, shouldn't you show a little more consideration for the victim's feelings?!"
"A-and besides!@k@vS04/36/310100629. Your methods are underhanded!@k@vS04/36/310100630. Even if his family consented, he himself never agreed to cooperate!@k@vS04/36/310100631. Isn't that right?!"
"Na-Natsumi-chan..."

With a flicker of irritation, Detective Minai swept her hair back and clicked her tongue softly. She let out a breath and began to turn away — but then her gaze caught my face, and something shifted in her expression.
"...You're quite protective of him.@k@vS04/28/310500161. Could it be that you're also... from Hinamizawa?"
A jolt of terror shot through my entire body. ......In that moment, I finally realized I'd thrust my hand into a hornet's nest.
If I had just kept quiet and looked the other way.
If I had simply walked away like the other caregivers, telling myself it was none of my business.
Then this detective never would have asked me that question.
Why had I spoken up?
Why had I stepped forward?
If it came out here that I, too, was from Hinamizawa...... then I would lose my place here, just like him......?!

'Rumors are spreading that people from Hinamizawa have been disturbing others with their erratic behavior and delusions...@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1043. I thought it couldn't be true, but now that I've seen it...'
"...As I thought — you are too?"
changeReason: New translation removes hinamizawa, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "N-no — you're wrong!!@k@vS04/36/310100634. I-I'm not from Hinamizawa!!"
I heard it.
I heard it, and I couldn't unhear it.
Old man Hatakeyama's sharp intake of breath.
It... didn't sound like words, not really.
But it cut far deeper than words ever could... sharp as a blade, heavy as lead, carving into my chest...
"......I see.@k@vS04/28/310500165. I'm sorry — that was a strange thing to ask."
Detective Minai, who had been fixed on me for a long moment, abruptly let the tension ease and gave a slight bow. Then, lightly brushing her bangs aside, she smiled.

"I'm a little stuck on this investigation, you see.@k@vS04/28/310500167. ...The people of Hinamizawa won't cooperate with the police at all.@k@vS04/28/310500168. I was worked up, and it wasn't fair to put that on you."

"You're right.@k@vS04/28/310500170. Jumping to conclusions and asking someone point-blank if they're from Hinamizawa — that wasn't appropriate.@k@vS04/28/310500172. I'd be grateful if you could forgive me."
"...It's fine, really..."
I wasn't angry because she'd jumped to conclusions. It was more...
That was what I wanted to say. That one thing, at least, I wanted to make clear.
But... I couldn't.
Having already told the lie, I no longer had the right to take it back...
"......Excuse me."
I gave a brief bow and left the room without looking back.
I couldn't bring myself to look at old man Hatakeyama's face...
The wall clock ticked, and its two hands stopped at six o'clock. A quiet chime sounded from its speaker... signaling the end of my shift.
The rain clouds must have detoured around this area, because the warm red light of sunset was streaming into the room. ...As if the storm had been chased away by all the commotion.
...After that, old man Hatakeyama was struck by a sudden fever and had to be admitted to the adjacent hospital. As for whose fault that was... I was probably the only one who truly understood.
Dispirited, I'd returned to the break room — and the door opened almost immediately. ...It was Yamase-san.
"Good work today, Natsumi-chan.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1045. Thank you, truly."
Unable to say anything more, I hung my head and fell silent. Yamase-san gently patted my shoulder and nodded again and again, wearing a kind smile.
"...I'm sorry.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1047. It should have been me, not you, who stood up to protect the people in our care."
"Yamase-san..."
Yamase-san bit her lip and hung her head, looking pained. This was the usual, gentle, warm Yamase-san.
"I'm the one who should apologize.@k@vS04/36/310100643. You'd already given them permission, and I barged in on my own...@k@vS04/36/310100644. Um... the detectives — they weren't angry, were they?"
"They might not have been in the best mood.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1049. ...But that doesn't matter.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1050. Protecting the lives of the people in this center comes first."
With a rueful half-smile, Yamase-san sat down in the chair across from me.
...Come to think of it, I'd heard that Yamase-san was summoned to an administrative meeting afterward. I wondered what they'd discussed...
"Actually...@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1052. at the meeting just now, they discussed transferring Hatakeyama-san to the psychiatric ward."
"——What?!"
I shot to my feet in shock, knocking my chair over backward. ...The psychiatric ward? Treating such a healthy old man as mentally ill — that was absurd!

"Yes, exactly.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1054. ...But as you saw, unfortunately, that kind of extreme behavior in a communal living environment can't help but be deemed unfit.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1055. So once his family is contacted and gives consent, he'll be hospitalized as early as next week."
'But,' Yamase-san continued.
"When you put yourself on the line to protect Hatakeyama-san earlier?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1057. Seeing that made me reconsider."

"Not everyone who comes to this center is without problems.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1059. Some come here for rehabilitation precisely because they couldn't make communal living work with their families."

"So our job is to help those people — to support and guide them until they can live with their families again.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1061. ...Somewhere along the way, I may have lost sight of something that important..."
"Yamase-san..."
"So... you know what?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1063. Natsumi-chan."
Yamase-san took my hand and wrapped it in both of hers. ...They were soft, warm, and wonderfully comforting.

"After you graduate from high school, please consider getting your caregiver certification.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1065. You — no, a girl with a heart as kind as yours is exactly the person who should be doing this work.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1066. I'll support you however I can."
"......Yamase-san...!"
I was happy.
So incredibly happy.
Because Yamase-san's words felt like the first ray of light illuminating the thing I'd been agonizing over most — my future.
changeReason: New translation adds yamase, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ......And yet...
I... couldn't bring myself to feel purely glad. Because I... had told a terrible lie to this woman who was nothing but kind...
Kind? Pure-hearted? ...There was no way. Absolutely not.
I was nothing but a liar, a fraud... a coward.
I didn't deserve to hear those words from Yamase-san... Not me...!
......Even so...

"...I'll do my best...!@k@vS04/36/310100652. I'll study hard to earn my certification, and I'll work to become more and more useful,@k@vS04/36/310100653. ...until I'm worthy of working alongside you someday...!"
Hearing that, Yamase-san nodded warmly.
Even if it was a lie. Even if it was hypocrisy, or whatever else... if it meant I could keep living peacefully in this city the way I always had......
The only thing I could do now... was stay committed to the lie... and keep working hard for others.
It had gotten very late again today. ...And so much had happened.
My body was utterly spent — heavy as lead. ...I wanted to get home and sleep. I didn't even need dinner — I just wanted to collapse into bed and sink into oblivion...
But... if I skipped dinner, Mom would probably snap at me again. So I'd drag myself to the table and mechanically shovel tasteless food into my mouth in a heavy, silent atmosphere.
Going home was depressing. If that was what awaited me... I didn't want to go home at all. I wanted to run away — to somewhere, anywhere, a world without suffering or pain...
Thinking that, I was about to pass through the main gate — when.
"Good evening. ——Natsumi-san."
A shadow approached with measured steps. ...It was the detective from earlier — Minai.

"Ahaha, please don't glare at me like that.@k@vS04/28/310500175. Questioning people that way — it's just how things get done in my line of work.@k@vS04/28/310500176. I truly do feel bad about making you uncomfortable."
"...Old man Hatakeyama developed a fever and was hospitalized.@k@vS04/36/310100660. That's on you."
Silence.

The affable smile she'd tried to put on faded, and Detective Minai's face went serious. I braced myself, thinking she'd be angry... but to my surprise, she quietly lowered her gaze and bowed her head.

"...I admit I went too far.@k@vS04/28/310500179. The investigation had stalled, and in my impatience I pushed too hard. That was my mistake.@k@vS04/28/310500180. I'm sorry.@k@vS04/28/310500181. I swear here and now — I won't do that again."
I'd never expected a straightforward apology. The harsh words I'd been about to hurl died in my throat. Unable to endure the awkwardness, I turned my face away and walked past her.
"...Goodbye, then."
"Yes — let's meet again sometime.@k@vS04/28/310500183. ——Natsumi Kimiyoshi-san."
Startled by that name, I stopped in my tracks without thinking.
When I turned around, Detective Minai stood with her arms crossed, facing me. Even in the dim light, her eyes glinted with unmistakable sharpness, fixed squarely on me.

"Your name tag... I could see it.@k@vS04/28/310500185. And 'Kimiyoshi' is one of the surnames belonging to the so-called Three Families — the most powerful old houses in Hinamizawa village. ...Isn't it?"
She knew...? This woman... what exactly was she trying to dig out of me...?!
"...Hey, Natsumi Kimiyoshi-san."
"What happened to Hinamizawa village — I truly am sorry about that.@k@vS04/28/310500188. ...But I can't simply back down.@k@vS04/28/310500189. Not on my pride as a detective."
"......What are you..."
Trying to investigate? — I tried to ask, but the words lodged in my throat like a plug of air, and my voice wouldn't come. Before I could recover, Detective Minai spoke, solemn and unyielding.
